quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 8, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Molecular Formula Determination
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Molecular Formula Calculations, Empirical Formula from Percent Composition, Molar Mass, Converting Empirical to Molecular Formula

questions.question: A white solid used in some herbicides is analyzed and found to contain 21.61% sodium, 33.31% chlorine, and 45.08% oxygen by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 106.44 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: Na = 21.61 g ÷ 22.99 g/mol = 0.940 mol; Cl = 33.31 g ÷ 35.45 g/mol = 0.940 mol; O = 45.08 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 2.818 mol. Divide by smallest (0.940): Na = 1, Cl = 1, O = 2.818 ÷ 0.940 = 3.00. Empirical formula = $\text{NaClO}_3$. Empirical mass = 22.99 + 35.45 + 48.00 = 106.44 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{NaClO}_3$.

questions.question: A pungent, colorless liquid is analyzed and found to contain 22.56% phosphorus and 77.44% chlorine by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 137.33 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: P = 22.56 g ÷ 30.97 g/mol = 0.728 mol; Cl = 77.44 g ÷ 35.45 g/mol = 2.185 mol. Divide by smallest (0.728): P = 1, Cl = 2.185 ÷ 0.728 = 3.00. Empirical formula = $\text{PCl}_3$. Empirical mass = 30.97 + (3 × 35.45) = 30.97 + 106.35 = 137.32 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{PCl}_3$.

questions.question: A pale-yellow crystalline solid is analyzed and found to contain 14.87% phosphorus and 85.13% chlorine by mass. The molar mass of the compound is determined to be approximately 208.22 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: P = 14.87 g ÷ 30.97 g/mol = 0.480 mol; Cl = 85.13 g ÷ 35.45 g/mol = 2.402 mol. Divide by smallest (0.480): P = 1, Cl = 2.402 ÷ 0.480 = 5.00. Empirical formula = $\text{PCl}_5$. Empirical mass = 30.97 + (5 × 35.45) = 30.97 + 177.25 = 208.22 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{PCl}_5$.

questions.question: A sample of a white mineral (corundum) is found to contain 52.93% aluminum and 47.07% oxygen by mass. If the molar mass of the compound is approximately 101.96 g/mol, what is its molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: Al = 52.93 g ÷ 26.98 g/mol = 1.962 mol; O = 47.07 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 2.942 mol. Divide by smallest (1.962): Al = 1, O = 2.942 ÷ 1.962 = 1.5. Multiply by 2: Al = 2, O = 3. Empirical formula = $\text{Al}_2\text{O}_3$. Empirical mass = (2 × 26.98) + (3 × 16.00) = 53.96 + 48.00 = 101.96 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{Al}_2\text{O}_3$.

questions.question: Spectroscopic analysis of a high-temperature gas reveals a suboxide of aluminum containing 62.77% aluminum and 37.23% oxygen by mass. If the molar mass of the gas is approximately 43.0 g/mol, what is its molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: Al = 62.77 g ÷ 26.98 g/mol = 2.327 mol; O = 37.23 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 2.327 mol. Mole ratio = 1:1. Empirical formula = $\text{AlO}$. Empirical mass = 26.98 + 16.00 = 42.98 g/mol ≈ 43.0 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{AlO}$.

questions.question: A pale-yellow solid used as an oxidizing agent is analyzed and found to contain 55.6% calcium and 44.4% oxygen by mass. The molar mass of the compound is determined to be approximately 72.1 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: Ca = 55.6 g ÷ 40.08 g/mol = 1.387 mol; O = 44.4 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 2.775 mol. Divide by smallest (1.387): Ca = 1, O = 2.775 ÷ 1.387 = 2.00. Empirical formula = $\text{CaO}_2$. Empirical mass = 40.08 + 32.00 = 72.08 g/mol ≈ 72.1 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{CaO}_2$ (calcium peroxide).

questions.question: A sample of a reddish-brown mineral used in solar cells and antifouling paints is analyzed. It is found to contain 88.82% copper and 11.18% oxygen by mass. If the molar mass of the compound is approximately 143.1 g/mol, what is its molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: Cu = 88.82 g ÷ 63.55 g/mol = 1.398 mol; O = 11.18 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 0.699 mol. Divide by the smallest value (0.699): Cu = 1.398 ÷ 0.699 = 2.00; O = 0.699 ÷ 0.699 = 1.00. Empirical formula = $\text{Cu}_2\text{O}$. Empirical mass = (2 × 63.55) + 16.00 = 127.10 + 16.00 = 143.10 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{Cu}_2\text{O}$ (copper(I) oxide).

questions.question: A black, powdery solid often used in chemical batteries and as a precursor to wood preservatives is analyzed. It contains 79.89% copper and 20.11% oxygen by mass. If the molar mass is approximately 79.55 g/mol, what is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: Cu = 79.89 g ÷ 63.55 g/mol = 1.257 mol; O = 20.11 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 1.257 mol. The mole ratio is 1:1. Empirical formula = $\text{CuO}$. Empirical mass = 63.55 + 16.00 = 79.55 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{CuO}$ (copper(II) oxide).

questions.question: A pale-green crystalline solid is analyzed and found to contain 44.06% iron and 55.94% chlorine by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 126.75 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: Fe = 44.06 g ÷ 55.85 g/mol = 0.789 mol; Cl = 55.94 g ÷ 35.45 g/mol = 1.578 mol. Divide by the smallest value (0.789): Fe = 1, Cl = 1.578 ÷ 0.789 = 2.00. Empirical formula = $\text{FeCl}_2$. Empirical mass = 55.85 + (2 × 35.45) = 55.85 + 70.90 = 126.75 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{FeCl}_2$ (iron(II) chloride).

questions.question: A sample of a colorless, fuming liquid is analyzed and found to contain 22.56% phosphorus and 77.44% chlorine by mass. If the molar mass of the compound is approximately 137.3 g/mol, what is its molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: P = 22.56 g ÷ 30.97 g/mol = 0.728 mol; Cl = 77.44 g ÷ 35.45 g/mol = 2.185 mol. Divide by the smallest value (0.728): P = 1, Cl = 2.185 ÷ 0.728 = 3.00. Empirical formula = $\text{PCl}_3$. Empirical mass = 30.97 + (3 × 35.45) = 30.97 + 106.35 = 137.32 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{PCl}_3$ (phosphorus trichloride).

questions.question: A sample of a pale-yellow crystalline solid is analyzed and found to consist of 14.87% phosphorus and 85.13% chlorine by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 208.2 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g sample: P = 14.87 g ÷ 30.97 g/mol = 0.480 mol; Cl = 85.13 g ÷ 35.45 g/mol = 2.402 mol. Divide by the smallest value (0.480): P = 1, Cl = 2.402 ÷ 0.480 = 5.00. Empirical formula = $\text{PCl}_5$. Empirical mass = 30.97 + (5 × 35.45) = 30.97 + 177.25 = 208.22 g/mol, matching the given molar mass. The molecular formula is $\text{PCl}_5$ (phosphorus pentachloride).

questions.question: A highly reactive, pale-grey solid is analyzed and found to contain 83.0% potassium and 17.0% oxygen by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 94.2 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: K = 83.0 g ÷ 39.10 g/mol = 2.123 mol; O = 17.0 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 1.063 mol. Divide by smallest (1.063): K = 2.123 ÷ 1.063 = 2.00; O = 1. Empirical formula = $\text{K}_2\text{O}$. Empirical mass = (2 × 39.10) + 16.00 = 78.20 + 16.00 = 94.20 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{K}_2\text{O}$.

questions.question: A yellow solid used in space shuttles to scrub carbon dioxide and release oxygen is analyzed. It contains 54.99% potassium and 45.01% oxygen by mass. The molar mass is approximately 71.10 g/mol. What is its molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: K = 54.99 g ÷ 39.10 g/mol = 1.406 mol; O = 45.01 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 2.813 mol. Divide by smallest (1.406): K = 1, O = 2.813 ÷ 1.406 = 2.00. Empirical formula = $\text{KO}_2$. Empirical mass = 39.10 + 32.00 = 71.10 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{KO}_2$ (potassium superoxide).

questions.question: A highly reactive liquid used as rocket fuel is analyzed and found to contain 87.42% nitrogen and 12.58% hydrogen by mass. The molar mass of the compound is determined to be approximately 32.05 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: N = 87.42 g ÷ 14.01 g/mol = 6.24 mol; H = 12.58 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 12.48 mol. Divide by smallest (6.24): N = 1, H = 12.48 ÷ 6.24 = 2.00. Empirical formula = $\text{NH}_2$. Empirical mass = 14.01 + 2.016 = 16.03 g/mol. n = 32.05 ÷ 16.03 = 2. Molecular formula = $\text{N}_2\text{H}_4$ (hydrazine).

questions.question: A pungent gas commonly used in fertilizers and cleaning solutions is found to consist of 82.25% nitrogen and 17.75% hydrogen by mass. The molar mass of the compound is approximately 17.03 g/mol. What is the molecular formula?
questions.explanation: Assume 100 g: N = 82.25 g ÷ 14.01 g/mol = 5.87 mol; H = 17.75 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 17.61 mol. Divide by smallest (5.87): N = 1, H = 17.61 ÷ 5.87 = 3.00. Empirical formula = $\text{NH}_3$. Empirical mass = 14.01 + 3.024 = 17.03 g/mol, matching given molar mass. Molecular formula = $\text{NH}_3$ (ammonia).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered molecular formula calculations! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the relationship between empirical and molecular formulas.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on finding mole ratios and calculating the multiplier n.
scoreMessages.0: Please review percent composition, empirical formulas, and how to determine molecular formulas from molar mass.
